title: The Bundle of Sticks
lines: A man had several sons who were always quarreling with one another.
He tried again and again to make them see how foolish such conduct was.
At last he asked them to fetch him a bundle of sticks.
When they had brought it, he asked each, in turn, to break it.
Each tried, but none could break the bundle.
Then the father untied the bundle and asked them to break the sticks one by one.
"My sons," said the father, "see how it is."
"As long as you stay together, no one can harm you; but once you separate, you will be as weak as these separate sticks."
